scope: M73-M76 tactical matchup factors for single-match v3 modeling; no score prediction, no win-draw-loss probability, no betting advice
upstream_data_note: data/thread_outputs/round32-m73-m76-v3-20260628/data-collector.json and .md were read after landing. The data packet provides confirmed fixtures, probable-not-official predicted XIs, formation estimates, availability conflicts and gates; T-75 official lineups, final injury minutes and complete same-source market chain remain missing.
source_files_read: 比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/A组/A组第二轮复盘.md
比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/B组/B组第三轮复盘.md
比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/C组/C组第三轮复盘.md

data_gaps: T-75 official lineups and actual starting formations missing; all predicted XIs remain probable_not_official
final injuries, suspensions and minute restrictions missing
official knockout match technical reports and referee profiles missing
same-source odds snapshots are outside this tactical lane and were not used
South Africa and Paraguay final group-round postmatch data are less complete than B/C/E/F local postmatch material
double_counting_warnings: Do not add Canada transition upside twice if player_state already rewards David, Buchanan or Davies availability; cap Canada wide/transition positives until T-75 confirms Davies/Eustaquio/Kone minutes.
Do not extrapolate Germany 7-1 Curacao attack into both attack score and pressing/set-piece factors; use this packet mainly to shape phase-specific caps.
Paraguay set-piece and low-block value may already be embedded in defensive rating after Turkey 0-1; cap extra defensive floor unless Almiron/Enciso/Gomez availability is confirmed.
Netherlands high ceiling after Sweden/Tunisia should not be duplicated in generic attack and favorite ceiling; this packet separates wide-channel, set-piece and game-state effects.
Morocco 4-2 Haiti should raise late bench/wide attack but also clean-sheet risk; do not count both as pure attack quality without adding transition-defense cost.
Brazil Vinicius/Bruno form from Scotland 0-3 is player_state-linked; use wide and transition factors as matchup shape, not raw scoring upgrades.
Japan defensive discipline and Suzuki form may already be in player_state; set-piece-defense risk should be a separate cap, not a broad defense downgrade.

matches.match: M73 南非 vs 加拿大
matches.tactical_summary: 南非的最优脚本是 Broos 式中低位压缩、Williams 稳定门前、Mokoena 管理二点和定位球；加拿大的最优脚本是 Marsch 式高强度压迫和纵向转换。对模型最关键的不是谁控球，而是加拿大核心可用性是否足以支撑连续压迫，以及南非能否把比赛拖入低事件节奏。

南非.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Ronwen Williams; Nkosinathi Sibisi, Ime Okon, Mbekezeli Mbokazi; Khuliso Mudau, Aubrey Modiba; Teboho Mokoena, Jayden Adams; Oswin Appollis, Thalente Mbatha; Lyle Foster.
key_roles: Ronwen Williams: goalkeeper leader and defensive-floor stabilizer
Teboho Mokoena: midfield heartbeat, second-ball and set-piece source
Aubrey Modiba / Oswin Appollis: wide release and delivery
Lyle Foster or Evidence Makgopa: outlet runner and forward reference
substitution_triggers: If trailing, add direct runner or forward reference and increase early deliveries.
If leading, protect the 4-5-1 shell and keep one counter outlet active.
If Canada press traps the first pass, earlier midfield stabilizer substitution is needed.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Williams and Mokoena availability mainly affects conceded xG floor and set-piece/second-ball xG, not high-volume open-play xG.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: A more defensive midfield lowers tempo and raises draw/low-event management; an extra runner raises transition volatility.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Fullback selection decides how much Canada can isolate wide channels.

rerun_conditions: Williams missing or limited
Mokoena missing or moved away from central control
Two or more attacking-line changes
Official shape shifts to a back five

pressing_adjustment.evidence: South Africa tactics packet describes medium pressing and compact block first; A-group postmatch shows low-event survival value.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Small negative only; this is a choice to conserve structure, not a full defensive downgrade.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Williams/Mokoena can calm pressure, but Canada press can trap wide first passes.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Cap near neutral unless T-75 confirms conservative midfield and full-strength goalkeeper.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Tactics packet identifies counters, second balls and wide releases as main attack route.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Do not combine with a large open-play attack upgrade; this is situational chance quality.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Compact block and goalkeeper leadership reduce exposure, but chasing game state can widen shape.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Only raise risk if South Africa concede first or start a more attacking XI.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Canada have Davies/Buchanan-style wide acceleration when available; South Africa fullback spaces are a noted risk.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Limit magnitude if Davies is unavailable or minute-limited.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Mokoena gives central intelligence; Canada can match with Eustaquio/Kone if fit.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Official midfields decide sign; keep neutral until lineup refresh.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Mokoena delivery and South Africa's low-event equalizer profile support set-piece relevance.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Cap because complete taker-target map is missing.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Lower block reduces space behind but repeated wide recovery runs remain possible.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Positive means reduced risk; flip if South Africa must chase early.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Broos tournament pragmatism is stable, but bench attack ceiling is less certain.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: No large coaching penalty; use as stability marker.

game_state_tendency.evidence: A-group postmatch highlighted South Africa late resilience in survival-style low-event matches.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Apply to low-event and one-goal game states, not raw win probability.

加拿大.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Maxime Crepeau; Alistair Johnston, Moise Bombito, Derek Cornelius; Tajon Buchanan, Alphonso Davies; Stephen Eustaquio, Nathan Saliba; Jonathan David, Cyle Larin, Promise David.
key_roles: Jonathan David / Promise David: central finishing and bench impact route
Tajon Buchanan: wide runner and depth threat
Stephen Eustaquio / Ismael Kone: midfield release and press balance, availability-sensitive
Alphonso Davies: high-leverage wide outlet if fit, but local postmatch says he was not ready for Switzerland
substitution_triggers: If press intensity fades, add runner rather than slow controller.
If trailing, increase verticality and striker depth.
If leading, keep transition outlet active instead of pure passive shell.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Davies/Eustaquio availability materially changes xG creation and counterpress recovery; without them, Canada lose both chance speed and rest-defense quality.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: Full-strength Canada raises tempo; weakened midfield makes pressing more episodic and game-state swings larger.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Wide advantage depends on whether the left-side outlet is truly available.

rerun_conditions: Davies starts or is excluded
Eustaquio or Kone unavailable
Canada use two strikers from the start
Back line changes reduce recovery speed

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Canada tactics packet: Marsch identity, medium-high pressing and counterpress; local third-round review warns core absences reduce this.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Use conditional positive; halve if Eustaquio/Kone/Davies are not in functional minutes.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Central control can be good with Eustaquio but weakened in the Switzerland match when he could not start.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Do not add more than small positive before T-75.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Tactics packet marks transition attack high; Promise David scored as substitute against Switzerland.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Avoid double counting with player_state attacking form; cap if Davies absent.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Rest-defense exposure behind aggressive pressing is a standing Canada risk and worsened by midfield absences.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Negative means higher risk; feed into opponent counter/set-piece transition windows.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Buchanan/Davies profiles create wide isolation, while South Africa can be stressed in fullback recovery.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Davies availability is the cap switch.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Eustaquio/Kone uncertainty offsets technical midfield upside.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Canada set pieces are support weapon, not primary identity; complete map missing.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: High pressing and recovery races can expose the back line if first pressure is bypassed.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Link to South Africa direct-outlet chances rather than broad defense downgrade.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Marsch bench can refresh intensity, but health-driven substitutions can reduce structure.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Treat as slight volatility, not pure penalty.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Canada keep transition outlet alive when leading and become vertical when chasing.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Raises event volatility after first goal.

model_should_adjust: Canada transition and press are conditional on core availability
South Africa low-event resistance deserves a draw/low-tempo floor but not an open-play attack boost
red_team_should_challenge: whether South Africa's A-group survival signal is stale without final group-round data

matches.match: M74 德国 vs 巴拉圭
matches.tactical_summary: 德国仍有最高技术密度、前场反抢和多点攻击，但厄瓜多尔 2-1 德国复盘要求限制强队模板外推：若德国节奏管理下降，防守转换会被放大。巴拉圭更像 Alfaro 式低位、反击和定位球队；土耳其 0-1 巴拉圭证明其十人低位韧性，但美国 4-1 巴拉圭也暴露早丢球后结构散开。

德国.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Manuel Neuer; Joshua Kimmich, Jonathan Tah, Antonio Rudiger, Nathaniel Brown; Felix Nmecha, Nadiem Amiri; Florian Wirtz, Jamal Musiala, Leroy Sane; Kai Havertz. Neuer/Baumann and Sane/Undav are selection conflicts.
key_roles: Kimmich: build-up organizer and tempo controller
Wirtz / Musiala: half-space creators and press-resistance receivers
Havertz / Undav: box occupation and pressing reference
Sane / wide runner: depth and isolated acceleration
substitution_triggers: If Paraguay low block survives, add box finisher or second striker profile.
If Germany lead, reduce fullback exposure and protect counter lanes.
If Paraguay counters through Enciso/Almiron-type outlet, add midfield security.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Creator-heavy XI raises settled xG; striker/second-runner choice changes box-touch quality.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: Already-qualified tempo drag from group stage should not be carried blindly into knockout, but recent Germany control issues remain relevant.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: The more aggressive the fullbacks, the more Paraguay counter and set-piece transition routes matter.

rerun_conditions: Kimmich or first-choice central controller missing
Wirtz or Musiala rested/limited
Back line switches to three center-backs
Paraguay start two direct forwards

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Germany packet marks strong front pressing and counterpress; Paraguay prefer lower possession.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not stack with raw attack ceiling from Curacao 7-1; this is phase pressure.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Kimmich plus Wirtz/Musiala half-space receiving gives strong press resistance.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Reduce if central controller absent or Paraguay front two press aggressively.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Germany can convert front regains and second attacks; substitute depth noted after E-group reviews.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Cap because Ecuador match showed control and recovery can dip.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Ecuador 2-1 Germany exposed space when Germany could not close transitions after early lead.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Negative risk applies when fullbacks push and rest defense is thin.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Germany wide runners can pin Paraguay's fullbacks; Paraguay wide protection is a known risk after USA loss.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Do not overstate if Germany pick a narrow control XI.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Higher technical density and central creators versus Paraguay's lower-possession structure.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Use as tempo/progression edge, not automatic scoring conversion.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Germany have delivery and aerial quality; Paraguay also strong, so edge is limited.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Small edge only because full taker-target maps are missing.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: High line can invite Paraguay direct outlets, but Germany recovery quality remains above average.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Treat as conditional volatility rather than broad defensive downgrade.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Nagelsmann has multiple role-for-role attacking and midfield options; E-group notes highlight bench contribution.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Positive bench flexibility; cap if lineup rotation is heavy.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Group stage showed both high ceiling and tempo-management fragility.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Knockout reduces rotation drag but not transition-control risk.

巴拉圭.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Orlando Gill; Juan Jose Caceres, Gustavo Gomez, Omar Alderete, Junior Alonso; Miguel Almiron, Andres Cubas, Matias Galarza, Diego Gomez; Isidro Pitta, Julio Enciso.
key_roles: Gustavo Gomez / Junior Alonso: defensive organization and aerial duels
Miguel Almiron: transition runner if suspension/availability clears
Julio Enciso / Matias Galarza: counter shot and second-wave threat
Matias Rojas-type left-foot delivery: set-piece service
substitution_triggers: If level late, protect low block and set-piece outlet.
If trailing, add direct runner but accept higher defensive-line risk.
If card pressure rises, replace wide or defensive midfielder early.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Almiron/Enciso availability changes counter xG more than settled xG.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: A back five lowers tempo and raises set-piece importance; a 4-4-2 raises counter volume but adds gaps.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Center-back pairing determines whether Germany can sustain box entries.

rerun_conditions: Almiron suspension or availability confirmed
Enciso omitted or shifted wide
Paraguay start a back five
Two or more defensive starters changed

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Paraguay packet and Turkey match point to compactness after early lead, not full-match press.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Negative press does not equal weak defense; it signals lower active regain.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Low possession reliance and Germany counterpress can force hurried clearances.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Cap if Paraguay use back five and long outlet successfully.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Almiron/Enciso/Sosa profiles and USA/Turkey postmatch notes show counter route remains live.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Availability-dependent; do not duplicate individual player_state boosts.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: USA 4-1 Paraguay showed structure opened when chasing; Germany can punish disorganization.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Apply strongly if Paraguay concede first.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Paraguay wide protection was flagged after first-round collapse; Germany can overload both half-spaces.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Reduce if Paraguay select a back five.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Paraguay do not seek long central control; Germany's creators can pin the midfield block.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Model as territory loss, not automatic collapse.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Paraguay packet marks set piece as traditional strength and low-score breaker.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Cap because Germany also strong aerially and full map missing.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Low block reduces depth risk, but early concession can force unstable higher line.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Alfaro pragmatism helps structure, but chasing substitutions can reduce protection.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Turkey 0-1 shows low-block survival; USA 4-1 shows collapse if game opens.

model_should_adjust: Germany central/press edges are real but transition defense must stay in the tail
Paraguay set-piece and low-block survival need a separate upset/low-event branch
red_team_should_challenge: Germany overreward from Curacao 7-1 versus Ecuador loss context

matches.match: M75 荷兰 vs 摩洛哥
matches.tactical_summary: 荷兰的小组后两场显示边路、多点进球和三球尾部仍强；摩洛哥则以右路 Hakimi、替补冲击和防守压缩为核心，但 4-2 海地说明其干净零封假设变脆。核心对位是荷兰中后场推进与高点定位球，碰上摩洛哥右路转换和低位防守韧性。

荷兰.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Bart Verbruggen; Denzel Dumfries, Virgil van Dijk, Jan Paul van Hecke, Micky van de Ven; Frenkie de Jong, Ryan Gravenberch, Tijjani Reijnders; Cody Gakpo, Brian Brobbey, Crysencio Summerville.
key_roles: Frenkie de Jong: progression and tempo controller
Gakpo / Xavi Simons: half-space receiver and scorer-creator
Dumfries: width, back-post and crossing lane
Van Dijk: defensive-floor and set-piece high point
Brobbey / Depay / Summerville: box and bench/rotation scoring profiles
substitution_triggers: If Morocco sit compact, add extra box runner or dribbler around 60 minutes.
If protecting lead, retain one wide outlet and improve rest defense.
If Morocco attacks Hakimi side, adjust left-sided cover earlier.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Brobbey/Depay/Summerville choice changes box presence and late scoring probability.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: De Jong start supports controlled tempo; a more vertical front four raises event volume.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Dumfries and Gakpo-side choices decide whether Morocco's fullbacks are pinned.

rerun_conditions: De Jong missing or minutes-limited
Van Dijk/Gakpo unavailable after prior knocks
Netherlands use back three from kickoff
Morocco select a five-man defensive shell

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Netherlands can press mid-high and recent Tunisia match started fast.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not treat as constant high press; Morocco can bypass through Hakimi.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: De Jong progression and Van Dijk base improve resistance to Morocco's burst press.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: F-group reviews: Netherlands 5-1 Sweden and 3-1 Tunisia showed wide/multi-point acceleration.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Separate from generic attack rating; cap after two high-output matches to avoid overfit.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Netherlands packet flags midfield-behind and wide recovery; Morocco transition outlets are strong.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Key risk against Hakimi/Brahim/Rahimi counter channels.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Dumfries/Gakpo width can pin Morocco, but Hakimi creates a strong counter-edge.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Use as slight edge, not dominant.

central_control_advantage.evidence: De Jong and Simons give central progression; Morocco prefer compact defense over long control.
central_control_advantage.model_note: May not convert to high xG if Morocco block remains compact.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Netherlands high points and Van Dijk edge; Morocco are competent defensively but complete map missing.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Cap due to Morocco aerial quality.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Strong individual back line, but transition space was recurring risk after Japan draw.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Tie to Moroccan right-side outlet availability.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Recent scoring from Brobbey/Summerville/Depay-type roles supports bench/rotation value.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Tunisia 1-3 Netherlands showed early lead did not automatically kill third-goal tail.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Keep favorite third-goal tail alive without issuing score prediction.

摩洛哥.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Yassine Bounou; Achraf Hakimi, Chadi Riad, Issa Diop, Noussair Mazraoui; Azzedine Ounahi, Ayyoub Bouaddi; Ismael Saibari; Brahim Diaz, Bilal El Khannouss; Ayoub El Kaabi. Bounou has minor-knock watch.
key_roles: Achraf Hakimi: right-side progression and transition engine
Sofyan Amrabat: central screen and duel anchor
Brahim Diaz / Hakim Ziyech-type creator: half-space unlocking
Yassine Bounou: goalkeeper floor, but recent own-goal/high-pressure handling requires caution
Rahimi / Saibari: late and box-arrival scoring value
substitution_triggers: If level or leading, refresh wide runners and protect Amrabat zone.
If trailing, push fullbacks and add half-space creator, accepting counter risk.
If Dutch high points dominate, add aerial defender or deeper block.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Hakimi plus late attackers can lift transition and late xG even if possession share is low.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: Compact XI lowers tempo; chasing with fullbacks high increases BTTS/open-game risk.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Bounou/Amrabat/Hakimi availability decides defensive floor and escape quality.

rerun_conditions: Hakimi not starting or role reduced
Amrabat missing
Bounou replaced or fitness limited
Morocco start with a back five

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Morocco press in bursts but base identity is compactness and timed jumps.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Hakimi and central screen help exits, but Netherlands can trap slower circulation.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Keep near neutral unless official XI confirms strongest ball carriers.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Morocco packet and Haiti 4-2 show Hakimi, Rahimi and late runners can create in transition.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Avoid double count with Hakimi/Rahimi player_state; apply as matchup to Dutch transition-defense gaps.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Haiti scored twice and exposed clean-sheet assumptions when Morocco chased tempo.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Negative risk grows if fullbacks push high.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Hakimi right side is elite, but Netherlands can pin wide zones with Dumfries/Gakpo.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Model as asymmetric right-side edge, not whole-pitch advantage.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Morocco prefer compact defending and transitions; Netherlands have stronger sustained central progression.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Can still defend the center well; do not turn into large xG penalty.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Morocco have delivery and aerial quality, but Netherlands high points offset it.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Neutral until set-piece map and lineups are confirmed.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Compact low/mid block protects depth; risk rises only if chasing.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Positive means lower line risk in base game state.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Rahimi and Gessime Yassine late impact in Haiti match supports bench scoring path.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Use as late xG support, not starter-quality upgrade.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Morocco can lower tempo when ahead, but 4-2 Haiti shows chasing/goal-difference script opens the match.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Bifurcate leading compactness and trailing openness.

model_should_adjust: Netherlands wide/set-piece edge is real but Morocco transition prevents over-clean-sheet assumptions
Morocco late bench and Hakimi channel should be a separate counter branch
red_team_should_challenge: Are Netherlands outputs inflated by Sweden/Tunisia contexts
Is Morocco clean-sheet reputation stale after Haiti 4-2

matches.match: M76 巴西 vs 日本
matches.tactical_summary: 巴西在苏格兰 0-3 中通过 Vinicius 左路和 Bruno Guimaraes 肋部输送兑现早球/继续进攻尾部；日本则通过纪律、边路组织和瑞典 1-1 的比赛管理证明强队对抗和低事件平局能力。关键是巴西左路个人突破与日本整体压迫/补位的冲突，以及日本定位球防守高点劣势。

巴西.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Alisson; Danilo Luiz, Marquinhos, Gabriel Magalhaes, Wesley; Bruno Guimaraes, Casemiro; Lucas Paqueta; Vinicius Junior, Raphinha; Matheus Cunha.
key_roles: Vinicius Junior: left-side breaker and transition accelerator
Bruno Guimaraes: midfield balance and final-pass bridge
Rodrygo / Raphinha: inside-forward connector and opposite-side width
Marquinhos / Militao / Alisson: defensive axis and rest-defense floor
Neymar: availability/bench rhythm variable, not automatic full-match engine
substitution_triggers: If Japan compactness holds, add creator or second box runner.
If leading, manage star minutes while retaining left-side outlet.
If Japan counterpress wins central duels, add midfield balance.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Vinicius/Bruno starts sustain the left-half-space xG route; Neymar start or bench changes central creativity and tempo risk.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: Brazil can control after lead, but Scotland match shows they may continue chasing margin if space exists.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: Fullback aggression decides how dangerous Japan's counters become.

rerun_conditions: Vinicius or Bruno Guimaraes unavailable
Neymar starts and changes central structure
Brazil choose a double pivot instead of 4-3-3
Japan use back three with wingbacks

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Brazil packet expects selective medium-high press; Scotland match had early control from aggressive start.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Selective press only; avoid full-match press bonus.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Brazil midfield and center-back axis support pressure exits, though Japan press discipline is strong.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Moderate positive; Japan can still trap wide.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Brazil packet marks elite transition; Vinicius two goals and Bruno two assists versus Scotland confirm left/half-space route.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Cap with player_state to avoid duplicating Vinicius form.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Brazil fullback/attacker-heavy spacing can expose rest defense; Japan's first pass and wide runners are dangerous.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Use as Japan counter branch, not broad Brazil downgrade.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Vinicius left-side mismatch is Brazil's clearest destabilizer; Japan collective cover will be stretched.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: If Japan back three doubles wide lane, reduce magnitude.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Bruno and midfield balance give Brazil progression, but Japan's compact midfield and discipline limit dominance.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Brazil have size and delivery; Japan packet flags defensive set-piece/high-point vulnerability.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Do not stack with generic aerial superiority beyond this factor.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Brazil defensive axis is strong, but attacking fullbacks create counter windows.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Risk rises if Brazil chase with both fullbacks high.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Brazil bench depth allows role refresh; Neymar appearance versus Scotland was load-management signal.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Small late-quality support, not a scoreline tail by itself.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Scotland 0-3 showed Brazil did not stop after early lead in head-of-group context.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Keep favorite ceiling branch alive if first goal comes early.

日本.predicted_lineup_basis: Data-collector probable XI: Zion Suzuki; Takehiro Tomiyasu, Ko Itakura, Hiroki Ito; Yukinari Sugawara, Keito Nakamura; Kaishu Sano, Ao Tanaka; Daichi Kamada, Junya Ito; Ayase Ueda.
key_roles: Zion Suzuki: goalkeeper and late-save floor
Takefusa Kubo: right/half-space creator and press-resistant receiver
Daizen Maeda: running, pressing and box attack
Wataru Endo-type role absent/uncertain in local squad context; central screen must be confirmed
Wide wingback/fullback pair: cover against Vinicius and release counterattacks
substitution_triggers: If Brazil overload the left, add extra wide cover or switch to back three.
If trailing, add runner and raise counterpress but protect set-piece defense.
If level late, preserve compactness and avoid exposing central rest defense.

lineup_change_impact.xg: Kubo/Maeda availability controls Japan's counter and late-arrival xG; a back three may lower open-play xG but improve wide containment.
lineup_change_impact.tempo: Japan can accept lower tempo if level; trailing game state raises verticality.
lineup_change_impact.matchup: The left defensive chain against Vinicius is the T-75 must-rerun variable.

rerun_conditions: Japan start a back three instead of back four
Kubo omitted or moved centrally
Maeda not starting
Central screen or left-back changes against Vinicius

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Japan tactics packet and F-group matches show disciplined team pressing and compact jumps.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Brazil press resistance caps this positive.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Japan have short passing and rotation, but Brazil can press and isolate physical duels.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Use modestly; do not overrate against elite athletic press.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Japan packet marks high transition speed; Netherlands 2-2 and Sweden 1-1 show strong-side creation and late threat.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Apply mainly to Brazil fullback-space moments.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Team movement is strong, but Brazil's wide one-v-one pace stresses recovery chains.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Do not turn into a large defensive downgrade if back three starts.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Brazil's Vinicius channel is a major mismatch; Japan can mitigate through structure but not erase it.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Reduce if Japan select extra left-side cover.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Japan midfield discipline can slow Brazil; Brazil still have higher individual progression ceiling.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Near neutral; official central screen decides.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Japan packet explicitly flags defensive set-piece and physical high-point risk.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Keep separate from general defense rating to avoid blanket downgrade.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Back four can be stretched by Brazil speed; back three can reduce depth but lower counter outlet.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Moriyasu has credible shape-change options and game-state discipline, shown by Sweden 1-1 management.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Sweden 1-1 confirmed mutual-benefit/draw-management discipline and late goalkeeper intervention.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Use for low-event resistance if level; does not imply prediction.

model_should_adjust: Brazil wide/transition ceiling is high but rest-defense risk keeps Japan counter branch live
Japan set-piece-defense risk should be isolated rather than broad defense downgrade
red_team_should_challenge: Japan left-side containment plan against Vinicius
whether Brazil's Scotland early-goal script overstates knockout repeatability
